tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Memphis Community Builds New Home for Woman Who Slept in Her Car for Years
stories.summary: At 82, Christine Marshall moved into a newly built home on South Parkway after years sleeping in her car, thanks to contractor Jason Smith and nearly $80,000 raised by her son.

stories.headline: Ecuador Stuns Germany to Reach World Cup Knockout Round in Historic Comeback
stories.summary: Ecuador, winless and goalless through two games, stuns Germany with a late goal from Gonzalo Plata to reach the World Cup knockout round.

stories.headline: Chicago White Sox Welcome Children with Special Needs Onto Major League Field
stories.summary: White Sox players join Joliet Miracle League kids on the diamond, celebrating inclusion and the pure joy of baseball together.

stories.headline: Endangered Cyprus Vultures Breed Again After Decade of Conservation
stories.summary: Five breeding pairs of Cyprus vultures are nesting again, including four birds brought from Spain, marking their first successful reproduction since 2021.

stories.headline: Tiny Curaçao Earns Historic First World Cup Point With Tears of Joy
stories.summary: Curaçao's 78-year-old manager Dick Advocaat, who left to care for his ill daughter, returns to guide the tiny island nation of 156,000 to their first World Cup point.

stories.headline: Thai Island Triples Endangered Dugong Population Through Seagrass Restoration
stories.summary: By restoring seagrass meadows on Thailand's Koh Libong, volunteers have welcomed back dugongs to waters where the population had dramatically declined from starvation.

stories.headline: Lincoln Barber Trades Haircuts for Diaper Donations to Help Struggling Families
stories.summary: Barber Sam Dov is swapping haircuts for diaper donations through June, aiming to collect $10,000 worth to distribute to struggling Lincoln families through the nonprofit Lincoln Littles.

stories.headline: South Africa Stuns South Korea to Reach World Cup Knockout Stage for First Time
stories.summary: Cyprus-based winger Thapelo Maseko scores in the 63rd minute as Bafana Bafana reach the World Cup knockout stage for the first time in their history.

stories.headline: Seven Golden Langurs Released Back Into the Wild After Rescue From Traffickers
stories.summary: Endangered primates trafficked from the wild are successfully rehabilitated and released back into their natural habitat in Sikhna Jwhwlao National Park.

stories.headline: Teenagers on Bicycles Rescue Elderly Woman After 15 Hours Outside
stories.summary: Two 13-year-old boys found an elderly neighbor who had fallen and immediately got her help, proving heroism has no age limit.

stories.headline: Tamil Nadu's Forest Revival Welcomes Back Elephants, Deer, and Wildlife
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu restored over 40,000 hectares of forest by clearing nearly 31,000 hectares of invasive lantana, welcoming back elephants, gaurs, and ground-nesting birds.

stories.headline: Quilters Donate Handmade Comfort Blankets for Paramedics to Soothe Frightened Patients
stories.summary: The Stone Bridge Quilters, gathering for 26 years every Tuesday morning, donated 13 handmade quilts to paramedics to comfort frightened children and new mothers during emergencies.

stories.headline: Norwegian Researchers Develop Shape-Memory Capsule That Fights Ulcer Bacteria for 48 Hours
stories.summary: A clever new capsule unfolds in the stomach to deliver antibiotics 48 times longer than traditional pills, offering hope to millions.

stories.headline: Weak Cape Gannet Rescued and Released Back to the Ocean
stories.summary: A Cape Gannet found weak at Salt Rock beach was rehabilitated by C.R.O.W. and released back to the ocean by the NSRI Durban crew.

stories.headline: Basketball Star Giannis Antetokounmpo Serves Meals at Community Kitchen in Athens
stories.summary: Giannis and his brother humbly served alongside volunteers at a community kitchen, bringing smiles to those in need.

stories.headline: New Zealand's First Submersible Clears Invasive Seaweed 140 Times Faster Than Divers
stories.summary: New Zealand's $5 million underwater machine clears about 10,000 square metres of invasive caulerpa daily—140 times faster than divers could manage.

stories.headline: Engineers Create Artificial Neuron That Thinks Like a Brain With Minimal Power
stories.summary: McGill researchers created a light-detecting nanoscale device mimicking neuron behavior, requiring minimal power and promising revolutionary retinal implants and energy-efficient AI.

stories.headline: Phillies Stun Nationals With Another Ninth-Inning Comeback
stories.summary: Derek Hill, a Phillie for less than two weeks, delivers a game-tying home run with two outs and two strikes in the ninth inning for a second consecutive improbable comeback.
